title: Bulk report - Week 43
summary:
Capesize
The week in the capesize market concluded with a continued trend of declining rates. In the Pacific region, despite the addition of a second major player later in the week, trading remained lacklustre. Rates for C5 fell by a total of $2.20 throughout the week, reflecting the prevailing bearish sentiment.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The week in the capesize market concluded with a continued trend of declining rates. In the Pacific region, despite the addition of a second major player later in the week, trading remained lacklustre.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A moderate week for the Panamax sector, with a slower pace seen in both basins. Much of the focus in the Atlantic came ex-EC South America, with mid-week seeing a mini push for first-half November arrivals, with reports of an 82,000-dwt delivery Aps Santos early November achieving $19,000 + $900,000 perhaps the highlight.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: A subdued week overall with the Atlantic remaining positional in key areas such as the US Gulf, but the South Atlantic was finely balanced with a limited amount of fresh enquiry. From Asia, brokers spoke of very little fresh enquiry from the south with little demand from Indonesia.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The lone positive region this week was the US Gulf and US East Coast. Limited tonnage availability led to rumours of large handy's fixing around $20,000 for Petcoke cargo from Texas to the Mediterranean.
